textContent:
The heated fight between the powerul frailles just ended as they viewed the houses by the Pasig River vicariously. Fr. Florentino was asked to come upstairs immediately for a small talk.
Sibyla: Bad time, bad time
Irene: Don't say it vice-rector. We quickly earned tonnes of money in Hong Kong and the buildings we...
Sibyla: W-wait. Don't tell me you don't know how much we have spent in this! The taxpayers are even starting to object!
Camorra: Enough! Enough of the whining or else I might lose my temper. Didn't you all know that the Indios are starting to bargain every time I collect their taxes?
On the middle of the conversation, Simoun peeked form under the deck and was easily seen by the Franciscans.
Custodio: Where on Earth did you hide Simoun? You didn't see all the beautiful cityscapes along the Pasig River!
Simoun: Oh, please. I have seen so many rivers and landscapes in my lifetime and only the ones with legends might be worth to see.
Captain: Well, this river then has all the things you're asking for eh? There's the story of the Enormous Sacred Lodestone. It's said that long before the Castellans have colonised this country, spirits have lived under it. Now that we have colonised it already, bandits have inhabited the area. However, the spirits were disturbed by the bandits and lived inside their bodies. There's also this story about Dona Geronima which will be told by Fr. Florentino.
Sibyla: Oh, come on! Everybody knows this already!
But since Fr. Irene, Camorra, Ben Zayb and Simoun still don't know what it's all about, they told Fr. Florentino to talk about it.
Florentino: Once upon a time, there is a student who promised to a girl that he will marry her in the future. But, the student forgot about the promise and left the girl waiting for him to come back. Decades have passed and she was informed that her soon-to-be husband is now an archbishop who made her a cave which she can live inside. Later, she grew old and didn't die peacefully as she was still waiting for the archbishop to marry her.
Ben Zayb: What a lovely story! I'll publish that. So sentimental!
A small chit chat have happened again talking about nonsense. Fr. Salvi then decided to tell about The Miracle of Saint Nicholas.
Salvi: Long ago, all of the rivers and lakes are inhabited by crocodiles. Some were large enough, they can flip a boat upside down by slapping their tails repeatedly. They said that an asian who was not baptised passed by a church. Suddenly, a devil shapeshifted into a croc sank the boat down so he can bring and eat the asian in hell. However, the asian seeked help to Saint Nicholas and the crocodile petrified immediately. The pieces of the stone can still be seen on the river they said.
Ben Zayb: What an amazing story! So good this will and should be published! The form of the animals, fear of the asian and lots of bounty! It can be seen that the unbaptised asian, when in time of need (oras ng kagipitan) can be helped by a saint who he does not even worship. 
Sibyla: Bueno. Since that we're approaching the lake, and talking about legends, the captain certainly knows something, even a little, about this.
Ben Zayb: Come to think of this, where in this lake did a man named Guevarra, Navarra or Ibarra died?
All of them looked straight into the captain and then to the lake as if they were trying to find something.
Dona Victorina: Oh, really? Where is it? Maybe we can still find something in the water!
Captain: Look, 13 years ago, according to the KAWAL (di ko alam english eh) that chased Ibarra, he swam two miles and then will come out to catch his breath. Once the KAWAL see a head bobbing in the water, they will shoot it immediately.
Ben Zayb saw Simoun looking pale and though that he just got drowsy because of the rocking of the BAPOR. But, he didn't know that he was very nervous that he started something that can and may take him down.
title: Chapter 3 - The Legends

The heated fight between the powerul frailles just ended as they viewed the houses by the Pasig River vicariously. Fr. Florentino was asked to come upstairs immediately for a small talk.

Sibyla: Bad time, bad time

Irene: Don't say it vice-rector. We quickly earned tonnes of money in Hong Kong and the buildings we...

Sibyla: W-wait. Don't tell me you don't know how much we have spent in this! The taxpayers are even starting to object!

Camorra: Enough! Enough of the whining or else I might lose my temper. Didn't you all know that the Indios are starting to bargain every time I collect their taxes?

On the middle of the conversation, Simoun peeked form under the deck and was easily seen by the Franciscans.

Custodio: Where on Earth did you hide Simoun? You didn't see all the beautiful cityscapes along the Pasig River!

Simoun: Oh, please. I have seen so many rivers and landscapes in my lifetime and only the ones with legends might be worth to see.

Captain: Well, this river then has all the things you're asking for eh? There's the story of the Enormous Sacred Lodestone. However, the spirits were disturbed by the bandits and lived inside their bodies. There's also this story about Dona Geronima which will be told by Fr. Florentino.

Sibyla: Oh, come on! Everybody knows this already!

But since Fr. Irene, Camorra, Ben Zayb and Simoun still don't know what it's all about, they told Fr. Florentino to talk about it.

Florentino: Once upon a time, there is a student who promised to a girl that he will marry her in the future. But, the student forgot about the promise and left the girl waiting for him to come back. Decades have passed and she was informed that her soon-to-be husband is now an archbishop who made her a cave which she can live inside. Salvi: Long ago, all of the rivers and lakes are inhabited by crocodiles. Some were large enough, they can flip a boat upside down by slapping their tails repeatedly. Ben Zayb: Come to think of this, where in this lake did a man named Guevarra, Navarra or Ibarra died?

All of them looked straight into the captain and then to the lake as if they were trying to find something.

Dona Victorina: Oh, really? Where is it? Maybe we can still find something in the water!

Captain: Look, 13 years ago, according to the KAWAL (di ko alam english eh) that chased Ibarra, he swam two miles and then will come out to catch his breath. Once the KAWAL see a head bobbing in the water, they will shoot it immediately.
